Begin by creating a stored procedure named CleanInactiveUsers in MySQL to handle the task safely.
First declare variables to store error messages and the total count of affected rows.
Start a transaction block to ensure data consistency during the deletion process.
Insert records into the status_log table before deleting them from the users table.
Capture the current timestamp for the modification_date field in the log entry.
Select all user_ids where the status equals Inactive to prepare for logging.
Delete the matching records from the users table after logging them successfully.
Implement a handler for SQL exceptions to catch any errors during execution.
Roll back the transaction if an error occurs to prevent partial data loss.
Return the error message to the caller if the process fails at any step.
Commit the transaction only if all steps complete without raising an exception.
The total number of deleted records is returned as an output parameter for verification.
This stored procedure ensures that every Inactive user is logged before removal.
Noah
